commands.hypothesis: First live test of the new Markov state-conditional exit DPM (deployed in PR #267). Same calm-survivor entry as v16-calm-base, plus a per-tick lookup against the 5526-path matrix on filter `dd>-10 + sum_abs<60` (n=938, baseline P_win=0.522). Strict default thresholds: exit when P(profit at T+300) drops below 0.30, hold past TP when P > 0.85 AND price > entry. The matrix cell needs n>=10 historical samples or the rule abstains and the fixed 25%SL/10%TP runs. Hypothesis: the 14pp mean-median gap on v16-calm-base (median +8.27% vs mean -6.09%) is driven by the loss tail running to ~-25%. Markov should kill those positions at T+60-T+120 once their (age, bucket) cell shows P_win < 0.30, lifting the mean meaningfully without touching the median win path. Predicted: median ~+7-8% (slight dip from occasional false-positive exits), mean ~-2 to 0% (loss tail trimmed ~5pp). If median holds and mean recovers, this is a v16 successor.

The clean test for whether Markov's value comes from the early-exit alone or from the let-winners-run side. Same entry + TP/SL as v16-calm-base. Markov exit threshold at 0.30 (active). Hold threshold at 0.99 — effectively unreachable, so the fixed TP=10 always fires when hit. Hypothesis: median should be essentially equal to v16-calm-base (~+8%) since the win path is unchanged, but mean should improve from -6.09% to ~0% to +1% if the early-exit mechanic actually rescues the loss tail. If exitonly lands at median +8 / mean ~0%, the early-exit is the entire story — we ship this as the v18 promotion candidate and the hold-side becomes optional sugar. If median drops alongside mean improving, then exits are catching too many noise pullbacks and v18-strict's hold-side is doing real work.

Calibration boundary test. Exit threshold loosened to 0.40, hold threshold loosened to 0.80 — Markov fires more often on both sides. Hypothesis: aggressive exits will overshoot into the win path the same way v16-calm-be3 did at +3% (median crashed to +2.15% vs base +8.03%). Predicted: median drops below base (false-positive exits clip the win tail), mean improves further than strict (deeper loss-tail rescue) but at the cost of overall edge. If median crashes to <+3%, the strict thresholds are correctly calibrated and there's no headroom on the exit side. If median holds near base AND mean improves more than strict, we found a better calibration and v19 can iterate on it. Tighter kill criterion than other v18 variants — we expect this one to fail fast.
